title: Change Management and Authorization Workflows
checks: Verify requester is authorized (System Administrator or Business User)
Ensure technical and business assessment completed
Confirm Change Advisory Board (CAB) approval obtained
Validate ICT Operations Team ready for implementation
Check Quality Assurance and Business Users available for verification
recommended_actions: For Category A (Major changes): New system implementations, major upgrades, architecture changes, significant configuration changes
Category A approval: CAB + Head ICT + Business Owner required
For Category B (Standard changes): Routine patches, minor enhancements, configuration adjustments, pre-approved changes
Category B approval: CAB or delegated authority required
For Category C (Emergency changes): Security patches, critical bug fixes, system restoration, immediate business requirements
Category C approval: Emergency CAB or Head ICT required
Request submission: Complete change request form
Business assessment: Conduct business impact analysis
Business assessment: Create user communication plan
Business assessment: Develop business verification plan
CAB review: Approve, reject, or request modifications
Implementation planning: Create detailed implementation plan
Implementation planning: Complete backup and rollback preparation
Implementation: Be ready to execute rollback if needed
Emergency procedure: Conduct immediate quick impact assessment
Emergency post-implementation: Submit formal change request
Emergency post-implementation: CAB review and ratification
Business verification: Conduct user acceptance testing
notes: All changes to production must follow change management process
Regular CAB meetings for standard changes
Change windows scheduled to minimize business impact
Comprehensive testing required before production implementation
Periodic audits of change management process required
Quality gates: All documentation complete, approvals obtained, backups verified, rollback tested, communication sent, resources allocated
Escalation path: Level 1: Change Requester, Level 2: CAB Chair, Level 3: Head ICT, Level 4: Executive Management
Performance metrics: Change success rate, change-related incidents, implementation duration, approval cycle time, emergency change frequency
Continuous improvement: Regular process reviews, lessons learned sessions, process metric analysis, training and awareness, tool and template improvements

Documentation requirements: Change request form, technical specifications, impact assessments, risk assessments, implementation plan, rollback plan, approval records, implementation logs, verification results, post implementation review
Compliance requirements: Audit trail of all changes, segregation of duties, approval authority validation, testing and verification evidence, rollback capability demonstration
